retrieved_chunks.title: Chapter 33 Sub-Chapter 4.3 Housing Allowance
heading_path: 3.05 Housing Benefit for OJT/Apprenticeship3.05 Housing Benefit for OJT/Apprenticeship
retrieved_chunks.content:
Individuals pursuing an approved OJT or apprenticeship program are paid a monthly allowance (similar to the MHA paid for institutional training) as follows:

  • First 6 months: A monthly amount equal to the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Second 6 months: A monthly amount equal to 80% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Third 6 months: A monthly amount equal to 60% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Fourth 6 months: A monthly amount equal to 40% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)
  • Over 24 months: A monthly amount equal to 20% of the BAH rate for an E-5 with dependents where the employer is located (prorated for benefit level and/or less than 120 hours per month)

retrieved_chunks.title: Part 4: Chapter 13 - Miscellaneous Award Issues
heading_path: d.  Second or Subsequent 6-Month-Step Rates Payable
retrieved_chunks.content:
(1)  The claimant reenters an OJT program with the same job objective with a different employer after completing 6 months of training under the initial award and credit is granted for prior training. 

(2)  The claimant reenters an OJT program with the same job objective with the same employer after completing 6 months of training at a different location. 

NOTE:  When the claimant reenters the same OJT program and the employer grants credit for prior training of less than 6 months, the first 6-month-step rate is payable only for the remainder of the first 6-month period.  This does not apply to initial or reentry OJT with different job objectives.

retrieved_chunks.content: Chapter 1606 has many features in common with other benefit programs described in this manual.  The following is a list of M22-4 chapters which contain substantive rules that apply to chapter 1606.  In certain instances, the rules are not exactly the same for chapter 1606 as for the other programs.  These differences are explained in the indicated chapters.

heading_path: SUBCHAPTER I.  Processing Chapter 1607 Original Claims
f. Date of Claim in BDN.
retrieved_chunks.content:
(1)  Priority 1 cases for which an additional issue has arrived requiring further processing should use a 380 EP and a date of claim of 2/21/06. EP credit should be taken.

(2)  For priority 2 and 3 cases, the RPOs will use the actual date of claim but a date no earlier than 2/21/06.

retrieved_chunks.title: Policy Issues IMPLEMENT AS ADVISED
heading_path: SUBCHAPTER I.  Processing Chapter 1607 Original Claims
retrieved_chunks.content:
(1) The first priority are claimants who are entitled to Chapter 1607 benefits but not for any period already paid under a different benefit program. Also, they should not have an overpayment remaining from another benefit.

(2) The second priority are claimants who are currently receiving Chapter 1606 benefits.

(3) The last priority are claimants who have received Chapter 1606 in the past and are due an adjustment but are not currently in school.   The RPO should designate a holding folder in TIMS for these priority 3 cases.

retrieved_chunks.title: Policy Issues IMPLEMENT AS ADVISED
heading_path: SUBCHAPTER I.  Processing Chapter 1607 Original Claims
c. Procedures for Paying a Chapter 1607 Award With Chapter 1606 Overpayment.
retrieved_chunks.content:
(1) The VCE should capture the existing Chapter 1606

(2) (See Policy Advisory dated 6/9/06) The VCE should terminate the 1606 record effective the first day of eligibility under Chapter 1607, or the first day of an enrollment after that, just as with any case that involves 1606 periods being converted to Chapter 1607. Suppress the letter.

(3) Using the FIST command, process an 04E transaction. Use 1607 as the benefit type on the Ready screen. On the F25 screen, place an X after 04E-ESTABLISH/INCREASE A/R. The CODE/CLASS is 61C. The TRANS AMT is the amount of the 1606 debt created in step 2 above. The DATE is the current date. Upon ALT/ENT, the FIST is ready for the Senior to FAUT.

(4) Send the case to the Senior VCE to authorize the 04E transaction (FAUT). Immediately after authorization, the Senior should return the claim to the VCE.

(5)  (See Policy Advisory dated 12/6/06 and Policy Advisory dated 6/9/06) The VCE will process the 1607 award, inputting all enrollments to be paid. The VCE should certify all periods of enrollment possible. Suppress all BDN letters in the process and send PCGL AWD 23 instead to advise the claimant of the award of benefits. Return the claim to the Senior VCE for authorization.

(6) Once the Chapter 1607 award is authorized, the 1606 debt should be recouped and any balance due the claimant will be issued.

NOTE: The 1607 award should be done as soon as possible following the creation of the 1606 debt to prevent the claimant from receiving unnecessary collection letters from Debt Management Center.

results.query: What are the 7 categories on the M22-3 QA claims processing scorecard?
results.expected_answer: The 7 categories are: (1) Data Integrity (Questions 1-3) — claim type, date of claim, TIMS token; (2) Development (Questions 4-5) — proper development criteria, claim controlled; (3) Eligibility (Question 6) — service dates, character of discharge, separation reason, delimiting date, alternative elections; (4) Entitlement (Question 7) — months/days accuracy, previously used entitlement; (5) Payment (Questions 8-10) — enrollment accuracy, payment delivery method, fiscal transaction, school/program approval; (6) Notification (Questions 11-13) — appropriate letter sent, accuracy of information, proper recipients; (7) Systems Compliance (Questions 14-16) — TIMS documentation, BDN, LTS systems, file status, authorization status, appropriate signatures. There are also Administrative Questions that do not count toward the National Accuracy Rate.

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
This interface, although initialized from Chapter 33 LTS, is controlled via the implemented control methods utilized in the VADIR web service.
The interface is designed to utilize HTTPS with bi-directional certificate validation.  
Mutual TLS Authentication is used with VA issued certificates to identify and authorize server-to-server communications to a specific endpoint identified by a URL where your services reside. TLS encrypts the request and return messages, providing confidentiality and integrity between the endpoints.  
Current security requirements include a minimum of TLSv1.1 for network transport and SHA2 encryption inside the certificates. Additional restrictions and ongoing mitigation are in place as part of the CRISP process which maintains this application at a state acceptable with VA security standards.

retrieved_chunks.title: VADIR Chapter 33 Long Term Solution Web Service Interface Control Document v2.1.1
retrieved_chunks.content:
This software interface provides the necessary data exchange between VADIR and Chapter 33 LTS to facilitate the population and display the agreed upon data feed to support data required from VADIR to Chapter 33 LTS.  
VADIR uses the Simple Object Access Protocol (SOAP) protected by Transport Layer Security (TLS) between the Chapter 33 LTS and VADIR servers after mutual authentication has been established. Data transferred under this agreement includes Service Members 'military service information as well as information concerning their eligibility for, participation in, and utilization of several Education benefits which existed prior to implementation of the Post-9/11 MGIB program.  These included Chapter 30 (Active Component), Chapter 1606 (Selected Reserve), and Chapter 1607 (REAP) as well as utilization and payment information from the VBA Benefits Delivery Network (BDN).  
Details of data transferred and of the Extensible Markup Language (XML) schema file are included in this ICD and defined in section 5.
